document_name: Botswana National Health Quality Standards for Hospitals  Psychiatric Care

categories.category_title: Coordination of patient care

standards.standard_title: During all phases of care, there are qualified individuals responsible for the patient's care.

criteria.criterion_text: An appropriately qualified individual has clearly defined responsibilities and accountability for all aspects of the service.
criteria.guideline: The unit manager’s job description and qualifications will be reviewed during the personnel record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The individuals responsible for the patient’s care are designated.
criteria.guideline: The focus is on the core care-givers i.e. medical practitioners and nurses. Evidence is required in the form of personnel allocation lists, duty rosters and off-duty lists. It is important to assess the availability of medical practitioners, especially after-hours, including weekends and public holidays.

criteria.criterion_text: The individuals responsible for the patient’s care are qualified.
criteria.guideline: The lists mentioned above must be examined for evidence of adequate numbers of the various categories of personnel in relation to the type of patients, acuity levels, bed occupancy levels etc. It is important that there are sufficient numbers of psychiatric qualified personnel available on each shift – at least one per shift. Proof of current registration with the relevant authority, where applicable, must be available.

criteria.criterion_text: The individuals responsible for the patient’s care are identified and made known to the patient and other personnel.
criteria.guideline: Name badges should be worn by all personnel members at all times. The process for obtaining informed consent should include informing the patient of the name of the individual who will be performing the procedure for which consent is being obtained. Patient interviews will reveal whether personnel introduced themselves to patients.

standards.standard_title: Clinical practice guidelines are used to guide patient care and reduce unwanted variation.

criteria.criterion_text: Evidence-based clinical practice guidelines relevant to the patients and services of the organisation are available to guide patient care processes.
criteria.guideline: Sometimes such evidence is available for nursing practices only, in which case this will be scored PC. In the absence of guidelines this criterion will be scored NC.

criteria.criterion_text: Organisational and clinical leaders set criteria to select clinical practice guidelines.
criteria.guideline: Every effort should be made to identify and adopt guidelines that have been rigorously and scientifically developed, internationally accepted and adapted for local use.

criteria.criterion_text: Implemented guidelines define and establish admission criteria for the adolescent service.
criteria.guideline: For optimal patient care and cost-effectiveness of service delivery, patients should only be admitted to specialist patient care units when they require the services offered by these units. To ensure that only patients requiring these services are admitted to these units, admission criteria should be established and applied to each potential admission. Admission criteria should be developed in collaboration with all relevant services, i.e. organisations who will refer to the specialist unit, the specialist unit themselves and the organisations to which patients will be transferred from the specialist unit. It may be appropriate to include administrative personnel in the process. In the adolescent services, the criteria must also include age limitations on admission to the service.

criteria.criterion_text: The implementation of guidelines is monitored as part of a structured clinical audit.
criteria.guideline: Clinical audits cannot be done on ALL guidelines, but evidence must be available that the management of high cost, high risk, high volume and problem-prone conditions are considered for auditing. If guidelines are available but structured clinical audits are not done, this criterion is scored NC. Sometimes such evidence exists for nursing practices only, in which case this will be scored PC.

criteria.criterion_text: Guidelines are reviewed on a regular basis and updated when necessary.
criteria.guideline: Organisational policy should define the frequency with which guidelines are to be reviewed to ensure they reflect current practice. This should include updating of guidelines following major changes in best practice advice as well as routine review.

standards.standard_title: The delivery of services is integrated and coordinated amongst care providers.

criteria.criterion_text: Care planning is integrated and coordinated among all care providers.
criteria.guideline: Evidence of team-delivered care, multi-departmental patient care rounds, combined care planning forums, integrated patient records, case managers, etc. will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: The patients' clinical records are completed according to organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: ROOT This refers to the relevant forms/documents that should be present in the patient’s record, as well as the completeness of entries made in the record. For this purpose, the results of patient record audits should be taken into account. Therefore, the score of this criterion needs to reflect the aggregated average score of all relevant criteria in this document. If the doctors’ (or other health care practitioners’) clinical notes are not available in the patient records, this criterion is scored PC.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient’s records are up to date to ensure the transfer of the latest information between care providers.
criteria.guideline: Clinical professionals (medical practitioners, nurses, therapeutic support services, social workers, etc.) form part of the multidisciplinary/interdisciplinary team and should all contribute to the patient record. Records of multidisciplinary/interdisciplinary team meetings should be documented as evidence of integrated care. All patient care information will be obtained from notes made by each member of the above team. The clinical involvement of medical practitioners must be measured in all relevant sections of the service. The notes in the patient record will indicate whether the patient has been assessed (initial, repeat, follow-up) by the relevant care providers at intervals appropriate to the patient’s condition and whether these assessments have been recorded satisfactorily. Records must be available to all care providers to enable them to make the necessary notes and/or check information. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.  If the notes of doctors and other health care professionals are not available because they are kept separately in another location, this criterion will be scored PC.

criteria.criterion_text: Information exchanged includes the patient’s health status.
criteria.guideline: This includes both the patient’s general health status and mental health status. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: 5Information exchanged includes a summary of the care provided.
6Information exchanged includes the patient’s progress.

criteria.criterion_text: The author can be identified for each patient record entry.
criteria.guideline: In order to ensure that appropriately qualified individuals provide and record their activities related to patient care, names, signatures and designations must be available and the authors must be identifiable. Methods of verifying signatures and designations could include: Names and designations printed below signatures Stamps indicating names and designations Specimen signature lists in the patient records or in the relevant department The specimen signature lists must be up to date and include all relevant signatures and initials. Specimen signatures and initials must match the usual signatures and initials used in patient records. If signature lists are used, these should be archived for future reference. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit. If the signatures and designations cannot be matched to any of the systems mentioned above, this criterion will be marked PC or NC based on audit results.

criteria.criterion_text: The date and time of each patient record entry can be identified
criteria.guideline: Records must follow a chronological order to allow for a logical flow of information. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

categories.category_title: Assessment of patients

standards.standard_title: All patients cared for by the organisation have their healthcare needs identified through an established assessment process within appropriate time frames.

criteria.criterion_text: Written policies and procedures for assessing patients and their families on admission and during ongoing care are implemented.
criteria.guideline: A policy must be available which defines the scope of practice for each discipline, together with specific information required, procedures to be carried out and all documentation to be completed during the assessment of patients and their families. The provision of specifically designed assessment forms for each relevant discipline is considered as evidence of the implementation of such a policy. Where the required information, according to the patient’s presenting complaints/condition is not documented, the score will be adjusted accordingly. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Only those individuals permitted by applicable laws, regulations and professional registration perform the assessments.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit, which will focus on the legibility of signatures and the identification of designations/qualifications. The absence of notes made by the doctor in the patient record, or other relevant clinical personnel other than nursing personnel will affect the outcome of the audit process.

criteria.criterion_text: The scope and content of assessment by each discipline is defined.
criteria.guideline: It is accepted that the scope and content of assessment for each discipline is defined if the organisation makes use of standardised formats of clinical assessment forms for specific use by different disciplines. The absence of medical notes or other relevant clinical personnel other than nursing personnel in the patient record will affect the outcome of the audit process. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Assessments are performed within appropriate time frames and are comprehensively documented in the patient's records according to organisation policy
criteria.guideline: Time frames for the assessment of patients should be established for each discipline, for example, there should be an indication of which cases are urgent/or not priority cases. It is important to note that this criterion does not refer to the length of time that it takes to perform the assessment, but the time within which the initial assessment should be commenced and/or completed. These time frames should be stated in the policy framework. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The date and time of assessment can be identified.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: Each patient has an initial assessment which complies with current policies, procedures and guidelines.

criteria.criterion_text: Each patient admitted has an initial assessment which meets organisation policy
criteria.guideline: ROOT Documented evidence in the form of a detailed policy and procedure or guideline is provided. To ensure coordination of patient care, all members of the team should be included in the formulation of this policy framework, especially doctors and nurses. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a health history.
criteria.guideline: The health history must include any history of drug and alcohol use and any history of anoxic episodes and/or head injury. Compliance is measured in the relevant areas in the patient’s records. This criterion will be scored PC if medical practitioners’ notes or notes by relevant clinical personnel, other than nurses, are not available. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a physical examination.
criteria.guideline: If the doctors’ notes are not available this criterion is scored NC. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a nutritional assessment of the adolescent.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a psychological and behavioural assessment of the adolescent.
criteria.guideline: Where required, collateral information from outside sources, for example schools must be included in these assessments. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a developmental history and current functional assessment of the adolescent.
criteria.guideline: Where required, collateral information from outside sources, for example schools must be included in these assessments. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a social, cultural and where applicable, economic assessment of the adolescent and his/her family
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a genogram of family construction and functional assessment of the family.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment results in an understanding of all previous care and the care that the adolescent and his/her family is presently seeking.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment results in a diagnosis and the formulation of an individualised plan of care for the patient.
criteria.guideline: If the initial assessment reveals any concern that requires referral to the legal system (for example, neglect or abuse) these must be addressed in the plan of care and implemented according to legal requirements and organisation policy. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation identifies patients in pain during the initial assessment process.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation identifies those patient populations and special situations for which the initial assessment process is modified.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence in the form of a detailed policy and procedure or guideline is provided. To ensure coordination of patient care, all members of the team should be included in the formulation of this policy framework, especially doctors and nurses. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Those special patient populations receive individualised assessments.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The findings of assessments performed outside the organisation are verified on admission.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Any significant changes in the patient’s condition since the assessment performed outside of the organisation are noted in the patient’s record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: A process is in place to identify needs for discharge planning at the initial assessment
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: Healthcare professionals responsible for patient care collaborate to analyse and integrate assessment information.

criteria.criterion_text: Assessment findings are documented in the patient’s record and are readily available to those responsible for the patient’s care
criteria.guideline: There must be evidence that all clinical professionals dealing with the patient have contributed to the patient’s record. If the doctors' (or other health care practitioners') clinical notes are not available in the patient records, this criterion is scored PC. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patient assessment data and information are analysed and integrated by those responsible for the patient’s care.
criteria.guideline: Those involved in the patient’s care form the multidisciplinary team. The constitution of the team will vary for each patient and may include representatives from the medical, nursing, social work, psychology, occupational therapy, physiotherapy and other disciplines, departments or services. Information from all disciplines involved in the patient’s care should be considered when developing the treatment plan. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patient needs are prioritised based on assessment results.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient and/or his or her family participate in the decisions regarding the priority needs to be met.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews and/or the patient record audits.

categories.category_title: Patient care
1Adequate resources are available for the provision of safe care to patients in the service.

standards.standard_title: The care provided to each patient is planned and written in the patient’s record.

criteria.criterion_text: The care for each patient is planned, provided and noted in the patient’s record.
criteria.guideline: The need for ongoing education and educational support must be included when planning the care of adolescents in the service. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient's response to care and therapeutic interventions is documented in the patient record.
Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.
Meetings to discuss patient care/treatment regimes are held by the multidisciplinary team at regular intervals as determined by organisational policy and documented in the patient’s record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: All procedures and diagnostic tests ordered and performed, are documented in the patient’s record.
criteria.guideline: This documented evidence may include nurses’ and doctors’ notes as well as printed reports, which are filed in the patient record. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The results of procedures and diagnostic tests performed are available in the patient's record
criteria.guideline: This criterion will be scored PC if the results are not initialled by the doctor, the treatment plan does not indicate action taken, or the nursing notes do not indicate that the doctor was informed. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Orders are found in a uniform location in patient records.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Only those permitted to write orders do so.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Reassessments are performed at appropriate, regular intervals and following a change in the patient's condition, and documented in the patient's record.

Patients should be reassessed according to clinical need. This may be continuous, hourly or daily (as a minimum), depending on the clinical condition of the patient. Any deterioration in a patient’s condition should result in a formal reassessment by an appropriately trained clinician – often a nurse in the first instance followed by a doctor if clinically indicated. The nature of these reassessments and the processes to be followed should be defined in organisation or departmental policy. If the doctors’ (or other health care practitioners’) clinical notes are not available in the patient’s records, this criterion will be scored PC. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.
Care plans are revised when necessary in response to the findings of reassessments.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: Each patient participates in a structured treatment plan.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence of regular psychotherapeutic interviews as indicated by the programme and individual patient’s needs.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: There is documented participation in a structured therapeutic programme six to seven hours per day.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a range of therapeutic activities available, according to the identified needs of the patient.
criteria.guideline: The unit should document all therapeutic activities available to patients together with relevant information of how to access these activities, for example, schedules of when the activities are available, contact details of service providers, activities offered by organisations outside the organisation for example, local NGO’s or other service providers, referral documents, etc.

criteria.criterion_text: There is documented participation of the patient with his or her family or significant other(s) in group therapy, as appropriate.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient has the least restrictive environment possible, with any restrictions placed upon him/her written into the treatment plan.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit and the survey visit to the unit.

criteria.criterion_text: Implemented policies and procedures guide the freedom of movement within the facility and weekend or other leave from the organisation.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: Compassionate care is provided to the dying and to those in pain.

criteria.criterion_text: The assessment process makes provision for patients in pain to be identified.
criteria.guideline: This requirement should be included in the assessment policy and procedure for the organisation and must include methods for identifying pain in non-communicating and intellectually disabled patients. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients in pain receive care according to pain management guidelines.
criteria.guideline: There must be evidence that all clinical professionals dealing with the patient’s pain management have contributed to the patient’s record. Monitoring of the effectiveness of pain management must be recorded. Evidence could include the documentation of pain using an individualised assessment form and pain scoring charts. Pain management continues with the prescription and administration of medication for pain as well as the follow-up assessment after treatment. These records will also be evaluated. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence that the effectiveness of pain and symptom management is monitored.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and families are educated about pain and pain management
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation has processes to educate health professionals in assessing and managing pain.
criteria.guideline: Training must include the identification of pain in non-communicating and intellectually disabled patients. Documented evidence of personnel training will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel provide respectful and compassionate care to dying patients.
criteria.guideline: This includes the organisation respecting the need of the physically ill or dying patient to be transferred to another suitable facility/unit. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit and patient interviews.

standards.standard_title: Policies and procedures guide the care of high-risk patients and the provision of high-risk services.

criteria.criterion_text: Documented policies, clinical guidelines or standard operating procedures for identified high risk patients and procedures, which include items (a)-(h) in the standard intent above as a minimum, are available and readily accessible.
criteria.guideline: The clinical and managerial leaders take responsibility for identifying the patients and services considered high risk, using a collaborative process to develop policies and procedures and training personnel in their implementation. Implementation will be verified during the patient record audit. The 40/80 rule for compliance will apply.

criteria.criterion_text: In-service training is provided to personnel to ensure they understand the intent and content of the policies and procedures.
Where policies and procedures refer to patient care, compliance will be measured against the relevant areas in the patients’ records during the record audit process.

By implication, if the policies are implemented, personnel can be considered to have been trained and this criterion will be scored accordingly. This training could take the form of formal in-service training, introduction at orientation to policies, discussions at departmental meetings, case studies, etc.
Patients receive care consistent with the policies and procedures.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit and patient interviews.

standards.standard_title: Adequate information is provided when obtaining informed consent from patients or their legal representatives.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a documented process for the obtaining of informed consent.
criteria.guideline: The documented process should outline all the steps in a) –o) in the standard intent above as a minimum. An assessment will be made of the provision of an organisational consent form and the completion of this form when indicated. This criterion will be scored NC if written consent is indicated but suitable forms are not used.

criteria.criterion_text: Consent forms are completed comprehensively and available in the patient records.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL This criterion refers to all instances where patients give informed consent. If consent was not required for the patient records audited, this criterion becomes NA. If there is a correctly completed consent form the implication is that the patient has been provided with the information indicated in the relevant criterion above. Where the form is not completed in accordance with policy, this criterion will be scored PC or NC, depending on the average results of the record audit (40/80 rule will apply). The relevant criteria above will receive the same score. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Verbal consent is obtained and recorded according to organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL  This criterion refers to all instances where patients give verbal informed consent. If consent was not required for the patient records audited, this criterion becomes NA. If there is a correctly completed verbal consent form the implication is that the patient has been provided with the information indicated in the relevant criterion above. Where the form is not completed in accordance with policy, this criterion will be scored PC or NC, depending on the average results of the record audit (40/80 rule will apply). The relevant criteria above will receive the same score. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and their families or decision-makers receive adequate information in a clear and understandable way to enable them to participate in care decisions.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a written policy guiding the consent for HIV testing which is specific to patients in mental health care organisations.
criteria.guideline: The presence of a mental health disorder does not automatically equal incapacity to consent to HIV testing. The policy must therefore address assessment for capacity to consent to HIV testing and the process to be followed if the patient is assessed as being incapable of giving informed consent.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a documented process for obtaining informed consent for the transfusion of blood and blood products.
criteria.guideline: The documented process should outline all the steps in a) – n) in the standard intent above as a minimum. An assessment will be made of the provision of an organisational consent form for the transfusion of blood and blood products and the completion of this form when indicated. This criterion will be scored NC if written consent is indicated but suitable forms are not used.

categories.category_title: Medication management

standards.standard_title: Medication is ordered according to organisational policy and stored in a secure and clean environment.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication is ordered according to organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: The organisational policy for ordering medication should be readily available to unit personnel. Documentation relating to the ordering of medication will be assessed for compliance to the organisational policy.

criteria.criterion_text: All storage areas for medication and pharmaceutical supplies comply with current pharmaceutical acts and regulations and manufacturer guidelines (for example, security, temperature, light and humidity control).
criteria.guideline: Note must be made of provision for medication that should not be exposed to direct sunlight. Where the ambient temperature in the cupboard/room cannot be maintained at 20-25 degrees Celsius there should be evidence that the environmental temperature is monitored and complies with the manufacturer’s requirements for medication storage.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication is stored in a locked storage device or cabinet that is accessible only to authorised personnel.
criteria.guideline: All medication storage areas (trolleys, cupboards, rooms, refrigerators etc.) will be examined for compliance. An unlocked refrigerator will only be accepted if it is in a locked room with access limited to relevant personnel only. An unlocked refrigerator in an unlocked room will be scored as NC.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication identified for special control by legislation or organisational policy is stored in a cabinet of substantial construction, for which only authorised personnel have the keys.
criteria.guideline: Country-specific laws and regulations or organisational policy will determine the nature of such medication.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication identified for special control by legislation or organisation policy is accurately accounted for.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL Compliance will be measured against the local medication control regulations. Control measures generally include the keeping of registers for these items.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication is securely and legibly labelled with relevant information as required by legislation and organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL Country-specific regulations apply, but must include at least the following: Name of ward and/or name of patient and patient record number (if applicable) Proprietary name/approved name or name of each active ingredient Direction with regard to the manner in which medication must be used Number of dose units in the container Date of dispensing Expiry date and batch number Additional labels with warnings and storage instructions in accordance with organisation policy

criteria.criterion_text: Medication is stored in a clean environment.
criteria.guideline: This applies to all medication storage areas in the organisation.

criteria.criterion_text: A dedicated refrigerator is available for medication requiring storage at low temperatures.
criteria.guideline: There should be a refrigerator designated for the storage of medication only.

criteria.criterion_text: The temperature of the refrigerator is monitored and recorded according to organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL Documented evidence in the form of daily temperature recording and monitoring of the refrigerator/s must be available. The temperature must be maintained within a safe range according to manufacturer’s specifications. This is usually 2-8 degrees Celsius.

criteria.criterion_text: Appropriate action is taken and recorded when the temperature of the refrigerator is outside the recommended range.
criteria.guideline: The actions taken must be documented and kept on file for reference purposes. Monitoring logs should demonstrate more frequent temperature checks until it is confirmed that the action taken has been successful in restoring maintenance of the required refrigerator temperature range.

criteria.criterion_text: Expiry dates (including those of emergency medication) are checked regularly at defined intervals according to organisational policy and medication is replaced before the expiry date.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured against relevant laws and organisation policies.

standards.standard_title: Medication use throughout the organisation complies with applicable laws and regulations.

criteria.criterion_text: Policies and procedures that guide the safe prescribing and administration of medication are implemented.
criteria.guideline: These policies should be developed by all role-players i.e. medical practitioners, nursing personnel and pharmacists. Country-specific regulations will apply. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Only those permitted by the organisation and by relevant laws and regulations prescribe medication.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Only those permitted by the organisation and by relevant laws and regulations administer medication.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a process to place limits, when appropriate, on the prescribing or ordering practices of individuals.
criteria.guideline: This is particularly relevant where certain medication may only be prescribed by designated specialists. Compliance will be verified with reference to organisational policy and country-specific legislation.

criteria.criterion_text: Relevant personnel are trained in correct prescribing, ordering and administration practices.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during personnel interviews and training records in personnel files.

criteria.criterion_text: On admission, all current medication taken by the patient is documented in the patient record, including herbal and over-the-counter medication.
criteria.guideline: Implementation of the organisational policy is required. This includes traditional and homeopathic medication. These should be re-prescribed on the prescription sheet if indicated. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Verbal and telephonic medication prescriptions are documented according to organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: The procedure to be followed must be clearly documented in line with current legislation and implemented. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence that patients are identified before medication is administered.
criteria.guideline: This will be assessed in relation to the existing policy framework, including the use of patient identification bands when required. Compliance will also be confirmed while observing medication administration practices and during personnel interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication is checked against the original prescriptions and administered as prescribed
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL Compliance could be assessed by observing the administration of medication while visiting the clinical departments. During the patient record audit process, check for evidence of transcribing by nurses from the original prescription onto the medication administration sheet.

criteria.criterion_text: The medication prescribed for and administered to each patient is recorded.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Healthcare professionals monitor medication effects on patients collaboratively.
criteria.guideline: Patient record audits should reveal whether the effect of medication was monitored, recorded and brought to the attention of the medical practitioner. The incidence and intensity of therapeutic and undesirable effects should be monitored. While clinical signs and symptoms are useful measures of drug therapy efficacy, additional information may be required, for example, plasma drug concentration may be used in the initiation and maintenance of certain medication.

criteria.criterion_text: Therapeutic plasma medication concentrations are checked in accordance with manufacturers’ guidelines and organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: While clinical signs and symptoms are useful measures of medication therapy efficacy, additional information may be required by utilising plasma medication concentration levels during the initiation and maintenance of certain medication. The organisational policy, manufacturer’s guidelines and medical practitioner’s orders identify those instances in which therapeutic medication levels must be obtained. Compliance is verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Adverse drug reactions (ADR) are observed, recorded and reported through a process and within a time frame defined by the organisation.
criteria.guideline: An adverse drug reaction (ADR) is an expression that describes harm associated with the use of given medication at a normal dose. The meaning of this expression differs from the meaning of “side effect", as this last expression might also imply that the effects can be beneficial. The study of ADRs is the concern of the field known as pharmacovigilance. Compliance is verified during the patient record audit. Evidence will be sought of documentation reporting the reaction to the physician and the pharmacist.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication errors are reported through a process and within a time frame defined by the organisation.
criteria.guideline: A process must be in place for the recording of medication errors. These include errors in relation to prescribing, dispensing and administration of medication.

categories.category_title: Food and nutrition therapy

standards.standard_title: Food and nutrition therapy appropriate for the patient and consistent with his or her clinical care is regularly available.

criteria.criterion_text: Food appropriate to the patient is regularly available.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified from interviews with personnel and patients, especially regarding times of meals and the availability of special diets. Menus and meal times will be checked.

standards.standard_title: 2Food orders appropriate for the patient’s nutritional status and needs are documented in the patient’s record.

criteria.criterion_text: Wherever possible, patient food preferences are respected and substitutions made available.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews.

standards.standard_title: 4Patient food preferences consider cultural and religious requirements.

criteria.criterion_text: When families provide food, they are educated about the patient’s dietary limitations.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: 6Patients assessed as being at nutritional risk receive nutrition therapy.

criteria.criterion_text: A collaborative process is used to plan, deliver and monitor nutrition therapy.
criteria.guideline: This should include the involvement of a dietician if available. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Nutrition therapy provided, whether oral, enteral or parenteral, is documented in the patient’s record.
Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.
Response to nutrition therapy is monitored and recorded.
criteria.guideline: This may take the form of weight/mass checks, laboratory tests for protein and/or lipid levels etc.

categories.category_title: Patient and family education

standards.standard_title: Each patient’s educational needs are assessed and written in their record.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and families’ educational needs are assessed and recorded.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a uniform process for the recording of patient education information.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and families are educated about participation in the care process.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews and patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and families are informed about the financial implications of care decisions.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and their families (when required) are educated about their diagnosis and relevant health risks.
criteria.guideline: For example, safe use of medication and medical equipment, medication and food interaction, therapeutic diet and food interactions, defaulting on medication use, etc. Compliance will be verified during patient interviews and patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation identifies and establishes relationships with community resources that support continuing mental and physical health promotion and disease prevention education.
criteria.guideline: Evidence of meetings will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and families are referred to these organisations as appropriate.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews and patient record audits.

standards.standard_title: Education methods consider the patient’s and family’s values and preferences.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient and family are taught in a language and format that they can understand.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews and patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: Those who provide education have the knowledge and communication skills for effective education.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews and personnel training records.

criteria.criterion_text: Health professionals caring for the patient work collaboratively when appropriate.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: 4Interaction between personnel, the patient and family is noted in the patient's record.

categories.category_title: Continuity of care

standards.standard_title: The organisation designs and implements processes to provide continuity of patient care services within the organisation and coordination among health professionals.

criteria.criterion_text: Policies and procedures that guide the movement of patients within the organisation are implemented.
criteria.guideline: This refers to transfers between departments within the organisation as well as admissions from the out-patient department. Please take note that a transfer to theatre/procedure room is also regarded as an internal transfer. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Individuals responsible for the patient's care and its coordination are identified for all phases of patient care.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Continuity and coordination are evident throughout all phases of patient care.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: When a patient is transferred within the organisation, they are accompanied by their patient record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patient handover between healthcare professionals is standardised according to organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: The complexity and comprehensiveness of the content of handover information required to achieve continuity of care will differ for different types of handover situations. The details to be included in each situation should be agreed upon by all stakeholders and formalised in the organisational policy framework to ensure a standardised approach at all times. The transfer of information can be verbal for low risk situations, but should be documented, preferably by the use of forms, for high risk situations. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: There is a process known to personnel to refer patients for specialised consultation, investigations and/or treatment at other healthcare organisations.

criteria.criterion_text: Policies and procedures that guide the movement of patients for referral to another organisation are implemented.
criteria.guideline: The organisation must clearly describe the referral process, especially where patients are sent to another healthcare organisation for specialist consultation or special investigations and then return to the referring organisation. Note that this criterion does not refer to the transfer of a patient to another healthcare organisation to take over the care of the patient. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients are accompanied and monitored by an appropriately qualified person during transfer.
criteria.guideline: This should include the cadre of personnel suitable for the level of care required by the patient to be transferred.

criteria.criterion_text: A copy of the referral note is available in the patient record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Follow-up care based on the findings of investigations and/or consultations performed outside the referring organisation is documented in the patient record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: There is a process to transfer patients to another organisation to meet their continuing needs.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a documented process for transferring patients to other organisations.
criteria.guideline: The documented process should be available in the unit and readily accessible to unit personnel. The process must include: Guidance on how to make the necessary transport arrangements The qualifications required in the clinical personnel accompanying the patient for various levels of clinical care

criteria.criterion_text: When a patient is transferred to another organisation, the receiving organisation is given a written summary of the patient's clinical condition and the interventions performed by the referring organisation.
Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.
A copy of the transfer summary is available in the patient record.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The transferring organisation documents the transfer in the organisation’s patient record.
criteria.guideline: Documentation must include as a minimum: The reason(s) for the transfer Any special conditions related to transfer The condition of the patient before transfer The name of the healthcare organisation receiving the patient Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The referring organisation determines that the receiving organisation can meet the patient’s continuing care needs, and establishes arrangements to ensure continuity.
criteria.guideline: This should be done by the departmental management team when referral pathways for the unit are established. These pathways should be documented along with all relevant details, for example, transport arrangements and contact details. The documented pathway should be readily available to personnel who will be responsible for making the decision to transfer patients to another organisation.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients are accompanied and monitored by an appropriately qualified person during transfer.
criteria.guideline: This should include the cadre of personnel suitable for the level of care required by the patient to be transferred.

standards.standard_title: There is an organised process to discharge patients.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a documented process to discharge patients.
criteria.guideline: This process must include any criteria or conditions that the adolescent and his/her family need to meet prior to discharge. The documented process must be available in the unit and readily accessible to, and known by organisational personnel.

criteria.criterion_text: Where the initial assessment indicates that discharge planning will be required, planning requirements are included in the patient's care plan and completed as scheduled.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation works with the family, caregivers, healthcare practitioners and agencies outside the organisation to ensure timely and appropriate discharge and continuity of care.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and where appropriate their families or caregivers are given understandable follow-up instructions which are documented in the patient's record.
criteria.guideline: The follow-up instructions may be provided to the patient by the medical practitioner, or by the nurse following instructions from the doctor. The instructions must include as a minimum: Medication information Possible complications Return for follow-up care When and where to obtain urgent care Compliance will be verified through patient record audits and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: A discharge summary, which includes (a)-(g) in the standard intent above as a minimum, is written by the medical practitioner when each patient is discharged.
criteria.guideline: This criterion and the next apply to the discharge summary completed by the medical practitioner and not to the nurses' discharge note. Where a discharge summary has only been partially completed i.e. does not include all items (a)-(f) it will be scored PC. A nurse's discharge note is not acceptable and is scored NC. The 40/80 rule for compliance will apply. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Each record contains a copy of the discharge summary.
criteria.guideline: This will ensure that the discharge summary is available for follow-up visits. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: When appropriate the patient is given a copy of the discharge summary.
criteria.guideline: This could be appropriate if the patient’s care will be continued outside of the country.

categories.category_title: Quality improvement

standards.standard_title: A formalised proactive quality improvement approach is maintained in the adolescent service.
1There is a written quality improvement programme for the adolescent service that is developed and agreed upon by the personnel of the service.

criteria.criterion_text: Indicators of performance are identified to evaluate the quality of treatment and patient care.
As the emphasis is on the quality of patient care, this cannot be compliant if there is no evidence of clinical audits.

These could take the form of evaluation of the implementation of clinical guidelines, case discussions, reports on negative incidents, etc. An indicator, in this context, is a measure used to determine improvements in clinical care over time. The information gained must be used to identify and implement improvement processes. Statistical reports without documented evidence of continuous quality improvement will be scored PC.
Processes are selected in order of priority for evaluation and improvement in the quality of treatment and care.
criteria.guideline: Quality monitoring is typically applied to high risk, high volume or high cost activities, or areas of concern identified by personnel, patients or visitors.

criteria.criterion_text: The quality improvement cycle includes the monitoring and evaluation of the standards set, and remedial action implemented.
criteria.guideline: The quality improvement cycle does not stop with the implementation of a quality improvement plan. Ongoing monitoring of the process or procedure under consideration is required, with evaluation of the monitoring data collected to ensure that the plan has achieved its intended aim. If not, the quality improvement cycle should be repeated, incorporating the learning from previous attempts, until the desired aim is achieved.

criteria.criterion_text: A documentation audit system is in place.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of such audits must be provided at departmental level. Where the documentation (patient record) audit is an organisation-wide multidisciplinary process, which includes all clinical departments, this criterion will be scored according to the findings of the involvement of each department in the process. Evaluation of results and remedial action taken must be documented as well as maintenance of achieved improvements over time.

criteria.criterion_text: An incident managing system for monitoring near misses/adverse events/sentinel events is implemented, which includes the documentation of responses to recorded incidents and interventions to prevent recurrence of the incident or minimise harm in the event of a recurrence.
criteria.guideline: Participation at department level in the organisation’s overall system of the monitoring of near misses/adverse events/sentinel events will be evaluated. Analysed data, with responses/remedial action related to departmental incidents are required.

categories.category_title: Patient rights
1The organisation is responsible for providing processes that support patient and family rights during care.

standards.standard_title: 3Measures are taken to protect the patient’s privacy, person and possessions.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel respect the rights of patients and families to treatment and to refuse
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients are informed of their right not to donate human tissue or to participate in research.
criteria.guideline: It is very important to ensure that this internationally accepted right is implemented in the mental health patient population.

categories.category_title: Prevention and control of infection

standards.standard_title: There is a comprehensive infection prevention and control programme encompassing patient care and employee health.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a programme, which is implemented, to reduce the risks of healthcare associated infections in patients and healthcare workers.
criteria.guideline: The programme must include as a minimum: Prevention of the spread of respiratory tract infections Prevention of the spread of urinary catheter related infections Prevention of the spread of infection through surgical wounds Prevention of the spread of infection through intravascular/invasive devices Safe injection practices, including single-use injection devices Care of the infected patient The wearing of personal protective equipment The disinfection of equipment Management of infected/soiled linen Management of hazardous waste

criteria.criterion_text: The department participates in the overall programme for quality management and improvement of infection control.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by evidence of departmental representation at organisational infection control meetings and activities and by the departmental contribution of data to the organisational infection control data collection systems.

criteria.criterion_text: The department identifies the procedures and processes associated with the risk of infection, and implements strategies to reduce risk.
criteria.guideline: The department participates in, and has documented evidence of, the identification of risks in their department. The strategies to reduce risk should be defined in policies and procedures and include the risks listed in this standard as a minimum. Such documentation must form part of the organisation-wide infection control processes.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel are trained in correct hand washing procedures.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of personnel training will be required, together with evidence of monitoring of implementation, for example, hand washing audits performed regularly according to organisational policy.

criteria.criterion_text: Organisational policy on handling, storage and disposal of healthcare waste is implemented.
criteria.guideline: The implementation of the organisation’s master policy will be evaluated in the department. The processes must include handling at source, collection, storage and disposal of healthcare waste.

criteria.criterion_text: Individuals who collect specimens are trained in the proper collection and handling of microbiological specimens.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of personnel training will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: The department provides education on infection control practices to personnel, doctors, patients and, as appropriate, family and other caregivers.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of personnel training will be required. Information pamphlets, posters, individualised education etc. will provide evidence of patient, family and other caregiver education

categories.category_title: Occupational health and safety and risk management

standards.standard_title: The organisation directs and controls risk management programmes, which include health and safety programmes that comply with legislation.

criteria.criterion_text: The department conducts ongoing monitoring of risks through documented assessments as part of organisation’s risk management processes.
criteria.guideline: The department participates in and has documented evidence of the identification of risks in their department. Such documentation must form part of the organisation-wide risk management processes. All relevant aspects and services of the department, for example, patient, personnel and visitor related risks, financial and legal risks, physical facilities, security and environmental risks, etc. should be included.

criteria.criterion_text: The Health and Safety representative for the department supervises implementation of the organisation’s health and safety programme.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: Fire safety measures are implemented.
criteria.guideline: The department’s participation in the organisation’s overall fire safety processes will be evaluated.

criteria.criterion_text: Relevant personnel are trained in the procedures relating to the reporting and investigation of near misses/adverse events/sentinel events.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of training required.

criteria.criterion_text: Security measures are implemented to ensure the safety of patients, personnel and visitors.
criteria.guideline: The department's participation in the organisation’s overall security plan for personnel and visitors will be evaluated.

criteria.criterion_text: Where vulnerable patients are cared for, special safety and security measures are implemented in accordance with organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: Some method of access control into the unit will be required for compliance with this criterion. Alarm systems can include intruder detection systems, security guards and panic alarms. Security guards must be monitored to ensure that they are providing an effective access control system. Alarm systems may be replaced by CCTV.

criteria.criterion_text: All personnel are trained regarding their role in providing a safe and secure patient care facility.
criteria.guideline: This is of particular relevance to the mental health care setting where all personnel must be alert to the safety and security of vulnerable patients. Documented evidence of training required.

categories.category_title: Coordination of Patient Care

standards.standard_title: Clinical practice guidelines are used to guide patient care and reduce unwanted variation
standards.intent:
Clinical practice guidelines provide a means for improving quality and assist practitioners in making clinical decisions. Consideration should be given to providing guidelines for high risk, high volume and high cost conditions as these are the areas that represent the highest risk to patients and the organisation. In addition, guidelines should be available for conditions which are rarely seen but may have severe consequences for patients if misdiagnosed or mismanaged.
Guidelines are found in the literature under many names including practice parameters, practice guidelines, patient care protocols, standards of practice, care pathways, etc.
Regardless of the source, the scientific basis of guidelines should be reviewed and approved by the clinical leaders and clinical practitioners before implementation. This will ensure that they meet the criteria established by the leaders and are adapted to the community, patient needs and organisation resources. Once adopted, guidelines should be reviewed on a regular basis to ensure their continued relevance.

criteria.criterion_text: Evidence-based clinical practice guidelines relevant to the patients and services of the organization are available to guide patient care processes.
criteria.guideline: Sometimes such evidence is available for nursing practices only, in which case this will be scored PC. In the absence of guidelines this criterion will be scored NC.

criteria.criterion_text: Organisational and clinical leaders set criteria to select clinical practice guidelines.
criteria.guideline: Every effort should be made to identify and adopt guidelines that have been rigorously and scientifically developed, internationally accepted and adapted for local use.

criteria.criterion_text: Implemented guidelines define and establish admission criteria for the child and family service.

For optimal patient care and cost-effectiveness of service delivery, patients should only be admitted to specialist patient care units when they require the services offered by these units. To ensure that only patients requiring these services are admitted to these units, admission criteria should be established and applied to each potential admission.Admission criteria should be developed in collaboration with all relevant services, i.e. organisations who will refer to the specialist unit, the specialist unit themselves and the organisations to which patients will be transferred from the specialist unit. It may be appropriate to include administrative personnel in the process.In the child and family service the criteria must also include age limitations on admission to the service.For optimal patient care and cost-effectiveness of service delivery, patients should only be admitted to specialist patient care units when they require the services offered by these units. To ensure that only patients requiring these services are admitted to these units, admission criteria should be established and applied to each potential admission.Admission criteria should be developed in collaboration with all relevant services, i.e. organisations who will refer to the specialist unit, the specialist unit themselves and the organisations to which patients will be transferred from the specialist unit. It may be appropriate to include administrative personnel in the process.In the child and family service the criteria must also include age limitations on admission to the service.
The implementation of guidelines is monitored as part of a structured clinical audit.
criteria.guideline: Clinical audits cannot be done on ALL guidelines, but evidence must be available that the management of high cost, high risk, high volume and problem-prone conditions are considered for auditing. If guidelines are available but structured clinical audits are not done, this criterion is scored NC. Sometimes such evidence exists for nursing practices only, in which case this will be scored PC.

criteria.criterion_text: Guidelines are reviewed on a regular basis and updated when necessary.
criteria.guideline: Organisational policy should define the frequency with which guidelines are to be reviewed to ensure they reflect current practice. This should include updating of guidelines following major changes in best practice advice as well as routine review.

standards.standard_title: The delivery of services is integrated and coordinated amongst care
standards.intent:
The coordination of patient care depends on the exchange of information between the multidisciplinary team. This can be through verbal, written or electronic means according to appropriate policies determined by the organisation. Clinical leaders should use techniques to better integrate and coordinate care for their patients (for example, team-delivered care, multi-departmental patient care rounds, combined care planning forums, integrated patient records, case managers). The process for working together will be simple and informal when the patient's needs are not complex.
The patient, family and others are included in the decision process when appropriate.
The patient's record contains a history of all care provided by the multidisciplinary team and is made available to all relevant caregivers who are authorised to have access to its content.
When a patient enters a ward or department, the specific information required and the procedures for obtaining and documenting it depend on the patient's needs and on the setting in which care is being provided.
The organisation defines, in writing, the scope and content of assessments to be performed by each clinical discipline within its scope of practice and applicable laws and regulations.
These findings are used throughout the care process to evaluate patient progress and understand the need for reassessment. It is essential that assessments are documented well and can be easily retrieved from the patient's record.
The healthcare organisation determines the time frame for completing assessments. This may vary in the different settings within the organisation.
40.2.1Criteria

criteria.criterion_text: Care planning is integrated and coordinated among all care providers.
criteria.guideline: Evidence of team-delivered care, multi-departmental patient care rounds, combined care planning forums, integrated patient records, case managers, etc. will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: The patients' clinical records are completed according to organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: ROOT This refers to the relevant forms/documents that should be present in the patient's record, as well as the completeness of entries made in the record. For this purpose, the results of patient record audits should be taken into account. Therefore, the score of this criterion needs to reflect the aggregated average score of all relevant criteria in this document. If the doctors' (or other health care practitioners') clinical notes are not available in the patient records, this criterion is scored PC.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient's records are up to date to ensure the transfer of the latest information between care providers.
criteria.guideline: Clinical professionals (medical practitioners, nurses, therapeutic support services, social workers, etc.) form part of the multidisciplinary/interdisciplinary team and should all contribute to the patient record. Records of multidisciplinary/interdisciplinary team meetings should be documented as evidence of integrated care. All patient care information will be obtained from notes made by each member of the above team. The clinical involvement of medical practitioners must be measured in all relevant sections of the service. The notes in the patient record will indicate whether the patient has been assessed (initial, repeat, follow-up) by the relevant care providers at intervals appropriate to the patient's condition and whether these assessments have been recorded satisfactorily. The records must be available to all care providers to enable them to make the necessary notes and/or check information. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit. If the notes of doctors and other health care professionals are not available because they are kept separately in another location, this criterion will be scored PC.

criteria.criterion_text: Information exchanged includes the patient’s health status.
criteria.guideline: This includes both the patient's general health status and mental health status. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Information exchanged includes a summary of the care provided.
criteria.guideline: If the doctors' (and other health care practitioners') notes are not available in the patient record, this criterion is scored PC. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Information exchanged includes the patient's progress.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The author can be identified from each patient record entry.
criteria.guideline: In order to ensure that appropriately qualified individuals provide and record their activities related to patient care, names, signatures and designations must be available and the authors must be identifiable. Methods of verifying signatures and designations could include: Names and designations printed below signatures Stamps indicating names and designations Specimen signature lists in the patient records or in the relevant department The specimen signature lists must be up to date and include all relevant signatures and initials. Specimen signatures and initials must match the usual signatures and initials used in patient records. If signature lists are used, these should be archived for future reference. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit. If the signatures and designations cannot be matched to any of the systems mentioned above, this criterion will be marked PC or NC based on audit results.

criteria.criterion_text: The date and time of each patient record entry can be identified.
criteria.guideline: Records must follow a chronological order to allow for a logical flow of information. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Assessment of Patients
All patients cared for by the organisation have their healthcare needs identified through an established assessment process within appropriate time frames
Written policies and procedures for assessing patients and their families on admission and during ongoing care are implemented.
criteria.guideline: A policy must be available which defines the scope of practice for each discipline, together with specific information required, procedures to be carried out and all documentation to be completed during the assessment of patients and their families. The provision of specifically designed assessment forms for each relevant discipline is considered as evidence of the implementation of such a policy. Where the required information, according to the patient’s presenting complaints/condition is not documented, the score will be adjusted accordingly. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Only those individuals permitted by applicable laws, regulations and professional registration perform the assessments.

Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit, which will focus on the legibility of signatures and the identification of designations/qualifications.The absence of notes made by the doctor in the patient record, or other relevant clinical personnel other than nursing personnel will affect the outcome of the audit process. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit, which will focus on the legibility of signatures and the identification of designations/qualifications.The absence of notes made by the doctor in the patient record, or other relevant clinical personnel other than nursing personnel will affect the outcome of the audit process.
The scope and content of assessment by each discipline is defined.
criteria.guideline: It is accepted that the scope and content of assessment for each discipline is defined if the organisation makes use of standardised formats of clinical assessment forms for specific use by different disciplines. The absence of medical notes or other relevant clinical personnel other than nursing personnel in the patient record will affect the outcome of the audit process. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Assessments are performed within appropriate time frames and are comprehensively documented in the patient's records according to organisation policy.
criteria.guideline: Time frames for the assessment of patients and their families should be established for each discipline, for example, there should be an indication of which cases are urgent/or not priority cases. It is important to note that this criterion does not refer to the length of time that it takes to perform the assessment, but the time within which the initial assessment should be commenced and/or completed. These time frames should be stated in the policy framework. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The date and time of assessment can be identified.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

categories.category_title: General

standards.standard_title: Each child and family has an initial assessment which complies with current policies, procedures and guidelines.
standards.intent:
The initial assessment of a patient is critical for the identification of their needs and initiation of the care process. A patient's social, cultural and family status are vital factors that can influence their response to illness and care. Families must be involved in the initial assessment to assist in understanding the patient's history, wishes and preferences.
Financial factors should be assessed as part of the social assessment, particularly when the family will be responsible for the cost of all or a portion of the care.
A functional, behavioural and nutritional assessment allows for the patient to be referred for specialist care if necessary.
Certain patients may require a modified assessment, for example, very young patients, the frail, those terminally ill or in pain, patients suspected of drug and/or alcohol dependence and victims of abuse and neglect. When appropriate, the assessment process should be modified to respect local cultural practices.
The outcome of the child and family's initial assessment should result in an understanding of the patient's medical and nursing needs so that care and treatment can begin.
Planning for discharge should be commenced during this initial assessment process.
When the medical assessment was conducted by a different healthcare organisation, a legible copy of the findings should be filed in the patient's record. Any significant changes in the patient's condition since this assessment should be recorded.

criteria.criterion_text: Each patient admitted has an initial assessment which meets organisation policy.
criteria.guideline: ROOT Documented evidence in the form of a detailed policy and procedure or guideline is provided. To ensure coordination of patient care, all members of the team should be included in the formulation of this policy framework, especially doctors and nurses. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes the health history of the child.
criteria.guideline: For children, the health history must include a summary of the mother’s pregnancy and delivery of the child and any history of anoxic episodes and/or head injury. Compliance is measured in the relevant areas in the patient's records. This criterion will be scored PC if medical practitioners' notes or notes by relevant clinical personnel, other than nurses, are not available. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a physical examination of the child.
criteria.guideline: If the doctors' notes are not available this criterion is scored NC Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a nutritional assessment of the child.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a psychological and behavioural assessment of the child.
criteria.guideline: Where required, collateral information from outside sources, for example crèches or schools must be included in these assessments. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a developmental history and current functional assessment of the child.
criteria.guideline: Where required, collateral information from outside sources, for example crèches or schools must be included in these assessments. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a social, cultural and where applicable, economic assessment of the child and family.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a genogram of family construction and functional assessment of the family.
