criteria.criterion_text: Patients have an anaesthetic assessment performed before the administration of anaesthesia by aqualified health professional.
criteria.guideline: Organisational policy must define who may perform this assessment and what the pre- anaesthetic assessment should include. The results of this assessment must be recorded by the individual who performs the assessment. Because the criterion refers to a medical assessment, it is scored NC if the patient record does not reflect notes by the person responsible for administering the anaesthetic. If only the nurses' assessment notes are available in the patient record, this criterion will be scored NC. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The medical assessment of patients for ECT is documented before anaesthesia.
criteria.guideline: Organisational policy must define who may perform this assessment and what the medical assessment should include. The results of this assessment must be recorded by the individual who performs the assessment. Because the criterion refers to a medical assessment, it is scored NC if the patient record does not reflect notes by a medical practitioner. If only the nurses' assessment notes are available in the patient record, this criterion will be scored NC. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The anaesthesia assessment determines if the patient is an appropriate candidate for the planned anaesthesia.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The name of the medical practitioner responsible for the procedure is documented.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients have the results of diagnostic tests recorded before anaesthesia.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: 6The anaesthesia assessment identifies any medication allergies/sensitivities.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients are re-evaluated before the induction of anaesthesia.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The anaesthesia care of each patient is planned and documented.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient, family and decision- makers are educated on the risks, potential complications and options of anaesthesia.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The anaesthesiologist or other qualified individual provides the education.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: Each patient's physiological status is monitored during anaesthesia and recorded.
standards.intent:
The anaesthetist monitors and records the physiological status of the patient during anaesthesia and enters the drugs and intravenous fluids used and the anaesthetic in the patient's anaesthetic record. The anaesthetist has access to the patient care notes and is familiarised with the findings of the medical examination. It is important that each health professional has access to the records of other care providers, in accordance with organisation policy.
41.3.2Criteria

criteria.criterion_text: The patient's physiological status is continuously monitored during anaesthesia.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The results of monitoring are entered into the patient's record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The anaesthesia used is entered into the patient's anaesthesia record.
Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.
Patient care notes are available to the anaesthesiologist.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: There is a system to monitor and document each patient's post-anaesthetic  status and to discharge the patient from the recovery area according to accepted guidelines.
standards.intent:
Physiological monitoring provides reliable information about the patient's status during the administration of anaesthesia and the recovery period. Monitoring methods depend on the patient's pre-anaesthetic status, choice of anaesthetic and complexity of the procedure performed during anaesthesia. In all cases however, the monitoring process is continuous and the results are entered into the patient's record.
Monitoring during anaesthesia provides the basis for monitoring during the post-anaesthetic recovery period. The ongoing, systematic collection and analysis of data on the patient's status in recovery may support decisions about moving the patient to other settings and less intensive services. Only a suitably qualified and experienced registered nurse or designated medical personnel may carry out monitoring in the recovery area. Recording of monitoring data provides the documentation to support discharge decisions.
The anaesthetist or other qualified individual decides whether the patient can be discharged from the recovery area to another level of care or from the organisation (as in the case of ambulatory anaesthesia). Standardised criteria developed by medical personnel are used to make discharge decisions. The decision to discharge the patient from the recovery area is recorded in the patient's record. The time of arrival and discharge from the recovery area are recorded. Signatures of those who handed over and those who received the patient are recorded.

criteria.criterion_text: The anaesthetist is responsible for supervising the recovery period.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients receive monitoring appropriate to their condition during the post- anaesthesia recovery period.
Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.
The qualifications and experience of personnel members who may monitor patients are documented.
criteria.guideline: These requirements should be documented in the personnel requirements section of the job description as part of the recruitment process.

criteria.criterion_text: Monitoring findings are entered into the patient’s record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient's physiological status is monitored during the post- procedural period.
criteria.guideline: This includes monitoring in the recovery room and in the ward following transfer. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Established criteria are used to make discharge decisions from the recovery room.
criteria.guideline: The criteria may be documented in a policy/procedure or printed on the recovery room section of the intraoperative record. A documented record of their application will be sought during the patient record audit. The policy must state clearly who assesses the fitness of the patient to leave the recovery area and what criteria are applied.

criteria.criterion_text: The anaesthetist applies the criteria and discharges the patient.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL  Compliance will be assessed during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The anaesthetist signs the patient out of the recovery area.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL  Compliance will be assessed during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Recovery area arrival and discharge times are recorded.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be assessed during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Signatures of those handing over and those receiving the patient are recorded.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be assessed during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Facilities, Equipment, Supplies and Medication

categories.category_title: Facilities, Equipment, Supplies and Medication

standards.standard_title: Facilities for safe surgical and anaesthetic care are provided and maintained.
standards.intent:
The design of the ECT treatment area provides space for the reception, anaesthesia, treatment, recovery and observation of patients.
There are areas for the disposal and collection of used equipment and waste, including contaminated waste and sharps. Safe and adequate storage space for pharmaceutical and surgical supplies is available including separate lockable cupboards for controlled medication.
Personnel are provided with office facilities or a day station, a restroom, washrooms and toilets.
41.4.1Criteria
The design of the ECT treatment area provides space for the reception, anaesthesia, treatment, recovery and observation of patients.
There is safe and adequate storage space for pharmaceutical and surgical supplies.
Access to the ECT treatment room is controlled.

criteria.criterion_text: There is access to disinfection facilities.

There may not be a sterilisation unit in the ECT treatment room. There must however be access to the required sterile supplies within the treatment area.There may not be a sterilisation unit in the ECT treatment room. There must however be access to the required sterile supplies within the treatment area.

Where resuscitation or critical monitoring equipment is used that does not have built-in battery backup units, there is an uninterruptible power supply (UPS) that complies with relevant requirements and regularly serviced and tested.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL This will be assessed in the ECT treatment room. It may be necessary to check with the maintenance manager regarding the servicing and testing of the UPS.

standards.standard_title: Anaesthetic equipment, supplies and medications used comply with the recommendations of local/international anaesthetic professional organisations or alternate authoritative sources.
standards.intent:
Anaesthetic risks are significantly reduced when appropriate and well-functioning equipment is used to administer anaesthesia and monitor the patient. Adequate supplies and medication must also be available for planned use and emergency situations. Each organisation understands the required or recommended equipment, supplies and medications necessary to provide anaesthesia services to its patient population.
Recommendations on equipment, supplies and medication can come from a government agency, national or international anaesthetic professional guidelines or other authoritative sources. There is an equipment maintenance programme.
41.4.2Criteria

criteria.criterion_text: The provision and use of anaesthetic mixture components complies with the guidelines for practice of the professional society.
criteria.guideline: Organisation policy should reflect the requirements of the country's professional body or another authoritative source.

criteria.criterion_text: The provision and use of breathing circuits complies with the guidelines for practice of the professional society.
criteria.guideline: An assessment will be made whether the most recent copy of the guidelines of the country's professional body or a copy of another authoritative source is available and is used to guide the choice of anaesthetic apparatus.

criteria.criterion_text: The provision and use of ancillary equipment complies with the
guidelines for practice of the professional society.
criteria.guideline: An assessment will be made whether the most recent copy of the guidelines of the country's professional body or a copy of another authoritative source is available and is used to guide the choice of ancillary equipment.

criteria.criterion_text: The provision and use of monitoring equipment complies with the guidelines for practice of the professional society.
criteria.guideline: An assessment will be made whether the most recent copy of the guidelines of the country's professional body or a copy of another authoritative source is available and is used to guide the provision of monitoring equipment.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication is available according to the requirements of the level of care provided.
criteria.guideline: Either the guidelines of the country's professional body or the national guideline on essential drugs can be used as the basis for deciding which medication should be available. Where such guidance is not available, the guidance from surrounding countries or international bodies should be consulted.

criteria.criterion_text: A medication trolley is available for the exclusive use of the anaesthesiologist in each theatre.
criteria.guideline: Each anaesthetist must have immediate access to all medication required.

criteria.criterion_text: Theatre personnel ensure that all equipment is included in the organisation's equipment replacement and maintenance programme.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required.

categories.category_title: 3Emergency and protective equipment are provided in the operating theatre.
4Recovery room facilities and equipment are available to provide safe and effective care.

Patient Rights

standards.standard_title: The organisation is responsible for providing processes that support patient and family rights during care.
standards.intent: This refers to the implementation of organisational policy on patient and family rights. Compliance will be verified during observation of patient care processes, patient record audits and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: There are processes that support patient and family rights during care.
criteria.guideline: ROOT  The organisation's policies will be evaluated against country-specific legislation, where applicable. The implementation thereof will be evaluated during the auditing of patient records, patient interviews and observation during a walk-about in the department. This applies to all the relevant criteria below.

criteria.criterion_text: There are processes to ensure that care is respectful of the patient's personal values and beliefs.
criteria.guideline: Implementation will be evaluated during the auditing of patient records, patient interviews and observation during a walk-about in the department.

criteria.criterion_text: Measures are taken to protect the patient’s privacy, person and possessions.
criteria.guideline: Confidentiality of patient information and patient privacy must be protected at all times. Standard operating procedures specific to the conditions and processes in the unit must be in place, known to relevant personnel and implemented. Likewise, security measures specific to the unit must be in place to safeguard the patients' person and possessions.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel respect the rights of patients and families to treatment and to refuse treatment.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients are informed of their right not to donate human tissue or to participate in research.
criteria.guideline: It is very important to ensure that this internationally accepted right is implemented in the mental health patient population.

criteria.criterion_text: Prevention and Control of Infection

categories.category_title: Prevention and Control of Infection

standards.standard_title: There is a comprehensive infection prevention and control programme encompassing patient care and employee health.
standards.intent: This standard measures the implementation of the organisational policy for infection prevention and control at unit level as well as the identification of unit-specific infection control risks. Personnel must be aware of the policy and there must be evidence that it is implemented throughout the organisation.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a programme, which is implemented, to reduce the risks of healthcare associated infections in patients and healthcare workers.
criteria.guideline: The programme must include as a minimum: Prevention of the spread of respiratory tract infections Prevention of the spread of urinary catheter related infections Prevention of the spread of infection through surgical wounds Prevention of the spread of infection through intravascular/invasive devices Safe injection practices, including single-use injection devices Care of the infected patient The wearing of personal protective equipment The disinfection of equipment Management of infected/soiled linen Management of hazardous waste

criteria.criterion_text: The department participates in the overall programme for quality management and improvement of infection control.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by evidence of departmental representation at organisational infection control meetings and activities and by the departmental contribution of data to the organisational infection control data collection systems.

criteria.criterion_text: The department identifies the procedures and processes associated with the risk of infection, and implements strategies to reduce risk.
criteria.guideline: The department participates in, and has documented evidence of, the identification of risks in their department. The strategies to reduce risk should be defined in policies and procedures and include the risks listed in this standard as a minimum. Such documentation must form part of the organisation-wide infection control processes.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel are trained in correct hand washing procedures.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of personnel training will be required, together with evidence of monitoring of implementation, for example, hand washing audits performed regularly according to organisational policy.

criteria.criterion_text: Organisational policy on handling, storage and disposal of healthcare waste is implemented.
criteria.guideline: The implementation of the organisation's master policy will be evaluated in the department. The processes must include handling at source, collection, storage and disposal of healthcare waste.

criteria.criterion_text: Individuals who collect specimens are trained in the proper collection and handling of microbiological specimens.
Documented evidence of personnel training will be required.Documented evidence of personnel training will be required.
The department provides education on infection control practices to personnel, doctors, patients and, as appropriate, family and other caregivers.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of personnel training will be required. Information pamphlets, posters,  individualised education etc. will provide evidence of patient, family and other caregiver education

criteria.criterion_text: Occupational Health and Safety and Risk Management

categories.category_title: Occupational Health and Safety and Risk Management

standards.standard_title: The organisation directs and controls risk management programmes, which include health and safety programmes that comply with legislation.
standards.intent: This standard measures the implementation of the organisational policy for occupational health and safety and risk management at unit level as well as the identification of unit- specific risks. Personnel must be aware of the policy and there must be evidence that it is implemented throughout the organisation.

criteria.criterion_text: The department conducts ongoing monitoring of risks through documented assessments as part of organisation’s risk management processes
criteria.guideline: The department participates in and has documented evidence of the identification of risks in their department. Such documentation must form part of the organisation-wide risk management processes. All relevant aspects and services of the department, for example, patient, personnel and visitor related risks, financial and legal risks, physical facilities, security and environmental risks, etc. should be included.

criteria.criterion_text: The Health and Safety representative for the department supervises implementation of the organisation’s health and safety programme.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: Fire safety measures are implemented.
criteria.guideline: The department's participation in the organisation’s overall fire safety processes will be evaluated.

criteria.criterion_text: Relevant personnel are trained in the procedures relating to the reporting and investigation of near misses/adverse events/sentinel events.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of training required.

criteria.criterion_text: Security measures are implemented to ensure the safety of patients, personnel and visitors.
criteria.guideline: The department's participation in the organisation's overall security plan for personnel and visitors will be evaluated.

criteria.criterion_text: All personnel are trained regarding their role in providing a safe and secure patient care facility.
criteria.guideline: This is of particular relevance to the mental health care setting where all personnel must be alert to the safety and security of vulnerable patients. Documented evidence of training required.

categories.category_title: 1During all phases of care, there are qualified individuals responsible for the patient’s care

standards.standard_title: 1An appropriately qualified individual has clearly defined responsibilities and accountability for all aspects of the service.

criteria.criterion_text: The individuals responsible for the patient’s care are designated.
criteria.guideline: The focus is on the core care-givers i.e. medical practitioners and nurses. Evidence is required in the form of personnel allocation lists, duty rosters and off-duty lists. It is important to assess the availability of medical practitioners, especially after-hours, including weekends and public holidays.

criteria.criterion_text: The individuals responsible for the patient’s care are qualified.
criteria.guideline: The lists mentioned above must be examined for evidence of adequate numbers of the various categories of personnel in relation to the type of patients, acuity levels, bed occupancy levels etc. It is important that there are sufficient numbers of psychiatric qualified personnel available on each shift – at least one per shift. Proof of current registration with the relevant authority, where applicable, must be available.

criteria.criterion_text: The individuals responsible for the patient’s care are identified and made known to the patient and other personnel.
criteria.guideline: Name badges should be worn by all personnel members at all times. The process for obtaining informed consent should include informing the patient of the name of the individual who will be performing the procedure for which consent is being obtained. Patient interviews will reveal whether personnel introduced themselves to patients.

categories.category_title: 2Clinical practice guidelines are used to guide patient care and reduce unwanted variation.
3The delivery of services is integrated and coordinated amongst care providers.
1All patients cared for by the organisation have their healthcare needs identified through an established assessment process within appropriate time frames.
2Each patient has an initial assessment which complies with current policies, procedures and guidelines.
3Pre-and post-anaesthetic assessments are documented.
4Healthcare professionals responsible for patient care collaborate to analyse and integrate assessment information.
1Adequate resources are available for the provision of safe care to patients in the service.
2The care provided to each patient is planned and written in the patient’s record.
3Each patient participates in a structured treatment plan.
4Compassionate care is provided to the dying and to those in pain.

standards.standard_title: 5The organisation has processes to educate health professionals in assessing and managing pain.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel provide respectful and compassionate care to dying patients.
criteria.guideline: This includes the organisation respecting the need of the physically ill or dying patient to be transferred to another suitable facility/unit. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit and patient interviews.

categories.category_title: 5Policies and procedures guide the care of high-risk patients and the provision of high-risk services.
6Adequate information is provided when obtaining informed consent from patients or their legal representatives.
1Medication is ordered according to organisational policy and stored in a secure and clean environment.
2Medication use throughout the organisation complies with applicable laws and regulations.
1Food and nutrition therapy appropriate for the patient and consistent with his or her clinical care is regularly available.
1Each patient’s educational needs are assessed and written in their record.
2Education methods consider the patient’s and family’s values and preferences.
Continuity of care

standards.standard_title: The organisation designs and implements processes to provide continuity of patient care services within the organisation and coordination among health professionals.
standards.intent:
As patients move through a healthcare organisation from admission to discharge or transfer, several departments, services and healthcare providers may be involved in providing care. Without coordination and effective transfer of information and responsibilities, errors of omission and commission are more likely to occur, exposing the patient to avoidable risks. The organisation should therefore document and implement procedures to minimise the likelihood of these errors occurring.
42.7.1Criteria

criteria.criterion_text: Policies and procedures that guide the movement of patients within the organisation are implemented.
criteria.guideline: This refers to transfers between departments within the organisation as well as admissions from the out-patient department. Please take note that a transfer to theatre/procedure room is also regarded as an internal transfer. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: 2Individuals responsible for the patient's care and its coordination are identified for all phases of patient care.

criteria.criterion_text: Continuity and coordination are evident throughout all phases of patient care.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: When a patient is transferred within the organisation, they are accompanied by their patient record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patient handover between healthcare professionals is standardised according to organisational policy.

The complexity and comprehensiveness of the content of handover information required to achieve continuity of care will differ for different types of handover situations. The details to be included in each situation should be agreed upon by all stakeholders and formalised in the organisational policy framework to ensure a standardised approach at all times. The transfer of information can be verbal for low risk situations, but should be documented, preferably by the use of forms, for high risk situations.Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.The complexity and comprehensiveness of the content of handover information required to achieve continuity of care will differ for different types of handover situations. The details to be included in each situation should be agreed upon by all stakeholders and formalised in the organisational policy framework to ensure a standardised approach at all times. The transfer of information can be verbal for low risk situations, but should be documented, preferably by the use of forms, for high risk situations.Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

categories.category_title: 2There is a process known to personnel to refer patients for specialised consultation, investigations and/or treatment at other healthcare organisations.
3There is a process to transfer patients to another organisation to meet their continuing needs.
4There is an organised process to discharge patients.
Quality improvement

standards.standard_title: A formalised proactive quality improvement approach is maintained in the forensic service.
standards.intent:
The senior management team is responsible for ensuring that standards are set throughout the organisation. Within each department or service, unit managers should ensure that standards are set for the particular unit. Departmental or service managers should use available data and information to identify priority areas for quality monitoring and improvement. This should be done in collaboration with the organisation's central quality management structures to ensure coordinated quality improvement activities throughout the organisation.
Quality monitoring is typically applied to high risk, high volume or high cost activities, or areas of concern identified by personnel, patients or visitors. Some examples of activities that may benefit from quality monitoring include:
Patient assessment
Procedures carried out
The use of medication
Medication errors
Patient and family expectations and satisfaction
The following will be evaluated:
The manner in which problems were identified and prioritised in this service for which quality improvement activities were initiated
The processes put in place to resolve the problems
The identification of indicators to measure improvement
The tool(s) used to evaluate these indicators
The monitoring of these indicators and corrective steps taken when goals were not achieved
Graphed and/or tabled results, as appropriate
A once-off project such as acquiring a specific item of equipment will be scored NC. Quality improvement processes not related to the clinical quality of patient care but to the environment within which care is provided, for example, monitoring the checking of the emergency trolley over time, will be scored PC.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a written quality improvement programme for the forensic service that is developed and agreed upon by the personnel of the service.
criteria.guideline: The minimum requirement for compliance will be the availability of evidence of: At least one clinical audit Participation in documentation (patient record) audits Participation in the monitoring of near misses, adverse events and sentinel events

standards.standard_title: 2Indicators of performance are identified to evaluate the quality of treatment and patient care.

criteria.criterion_text: Processes are selected in order of priority for evaluation and improvement in the quality of treatment and care.
criteria.guideline: Quality monitoring is typically applied to high risk, high volume or high cost activities, or areas of concern identified by personnel, patients or visitors.

criteria.criterion_text: The quality improvement cycle includes the monitoring and evaluation of the standards set, and remedial action implemented.
criteria.guideline: The quality improvement cycle does not stop with the implementation of a quality improvement plan. Ongoing monitoring of the process or procedure under consideration is required, with evaluation of the monitoring data collected to ensure that the plan has achieved its intended aim. If not, the quality improvement cycle should be repeated, incorporating the learning from previous attempts, until the desired aim is achieved.

criteria.criterion_text: A documentation audit system is in place.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of such audits must be provided at departmental level. Where the documentation (patient record) audit is an organisation-wide multidisciplinary process, which includes all clinical departments, this criterion will be scored according to the findings of the involvement of each department in the process. Evaluation of results and remedial action taken must be documented as well as maintenance of achieved improvements over time.

criteria.criterion_text: An incident managing system for monitoring near misses/adverse events/sentinel events is implemented, which includes the documentation of responses to recorded incidents and interventions to prevent recurrence of the incident or minimise harm in the event of a recurrence.
criteria.guideline: Participation at department level in the organisation’s overall system of the monitoring of near misses/adverse events/sentinel events will be evaluated. Analysed data, with responses/remedial action related to departmental incidents are required.

criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by evidence of departmental representation at organisational infection control meetings and activities and by the departmental contribution of data to the organisational infection control data collection systems.

criteria.criterion_text: The department identifies the procedures and processes associated with the risk of infection, and implements strategies to reduce risk.

The strategies to reduce risk should be defined in policies and procedures and include the risks listed in this standard as a minimum. Such documentation must form part of the organisation-wide infection control processes.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel are trained in correct hand washing procedures.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of personnel training will be required, together with evidence of monitoring of implementation, for example, hand washing audits performed regularly according to organisational policy.

criteria.guideline: The implementation of the organisation’s master policy will be evaluated in the department. The processes must include handling at source, collection, storage and disposal of healthcare waste.

criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of personnel training will be required.

categories.category_title: Occupational health and safety and risk management

standards.standard_title: The organisation directs and controls risk management programmes, which include health and safety programmes that comply with legislation.
standards.intent: This standard measures the implementation of the organisational policy for occupational health and safety and risk management at unit level as well as the identification of unit- specific risks. Personnel must be aware of the policy and there must be evidence that it is implemented throughout the organisation.

criteria.criterion_text: A designated security manager has responsibility for security within the forensic service.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: The department conducts ongoing monitoring of risks through documented assessments as part of organisation’s risk management processes.
criteria.guideline: The department participates in and has documented evidence of the identification of risks in their department. Such documentation must form part of the organisation-wide risk management processes. All relevant aspects and services of the department, for example, patient, personnel and visitor related risks, financial and legal risks, physical facilities, security and environmental risks, etc. should be included.

criteria.criterion_text: There is an implemented policy on fire safety and evacuation which is specifically adapted for the forensic service.
criteria.guideline: The policy must include as a minimum: The safety of patients and personnel during a fire Who has access to the fire alarm system and fire-fighting equipment The evacuation process and location of a secure area The department’s participation in the organisation’s overall fire safety processes will also be evaluated.

criteria.criterion_text: An implemented policy on the physical security of the forensic service ensures the safety of patients, personnel and visitors.

This must include as a minimum:Inner and outer security of the building and how they relate to each otherInspections of the inner and outer security of the buildingControlled access systems through all doors and gates that form part of the secured areaLine of sight and measures to address blind spots (staff placement, use of CCTV, mirrors etc.)How furniture, fixtures and equipment are secured to prevent using them as climbing aids, weapons, barriers, ligatures points etc.The security of windows and window frames (how they are set in the masonry, how they open etc.)The monitoring of security personnel to ensure that they are providing effective access control and security.The department's participation in the organisation’s overall security plan for personnel and visitors must also be evaluated.This must include as a minimum:Inner and outer security of the building and how they relate to each otherInspections of the inner and outer security of the buildingControlled access systems through all doors and gates that form part of the secured areaLine of sight and measures to address blind spots (staff placement, use of CCTV, mirrors etc.)How furniture, fixtures and equipment are secured to prevent using them as climbing aids, weapons, barriers, ligatures points etc.The security of windows and window frames (how they are set in the masonry, how they open etc.)The monitoring of security personnel to ensure that they are providing effective access control and security.The department's participation in the organisation’s overall security plan for personnel and visitors must also be evaluated.
Adequate security measures are maintained in patient accommodation areas.
criteria.guideline: These include as a minimum: Patient bedroom and bathroom doors are designed to prevent holding, barring or blocking The use of locks and over-riding of locks The safety of ventilation measures The standard of clothing provided to patients The control of cutlery and crockery The safety of light switches and electrical access points The use of observation panels

criteria.criterion_text: Implemented policies and procedures guide the procedural security of patients in the forensic service.
criteria.guideline: These policies must include as a minimum: Conducting searches of patients and their personal property The monitoring of prohibited, restricted and patient accessible items (including the risk associated with patient’s personal property) Anti-bullying (for those who are bullying and those who are being bullied) Managing mass violence in the forensic unit Liaison with law enforcement regarding incidents of criminal activity/harassment/violence Management of the patient’s use of electronic equipment and internet access (including appropriate use of social networking sites) Prohibited items and restrictive practices

criteria.criterion_text: An implemented policy guides visitor access to patients in the forensic unit.
criteria.guideline: This policy must include as a minimum: Documentation of the identity of visitors Searching of visitors Management of restricted items that may not be taken into the forensic unit by visitors (including secure storage facilities for personal items) Management of items brought by visitors intended for patient use and consumption Management of child visitors, unwanted visitors and visitors who pose a threat to patients and personnel The location and security of the visitor’s room/area

criteria.criterion_text: A policy on key/electronic pass management is implemented.
criteria.guideline: The policy must include as a minimum: The process for issuing all keys/passes in the forensic service (identification checks, security checks etc.) The replacement of lost keys/passes The security of spare keys/passes How keys/passes are secured to the personnel’s clothing

criteria.criterion_text: There are clear and effective systems for communication and handover of all security related issues.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of training required.

criteria.criterion_text: All personnel are trained regarding their role in providing a safe and secure patient care facility.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of training required.

Assessment of Patients

standards.standard_title: All patients cared for by the organisation have their healthcare needs identified through an established assessment process within appropriate time frames.

criteria.criterion_text: Written policies and procedures for assessing patients and their families on admission and during ongoing care are implemented.
criteria.guideline: ROOT  A policy must be available which defines the scope of practice for each discipline, together with specific information required, procedures to be carried out and all documentation to be completed during the assessment of patients and their families. The provision of specifically designed assessment forms for each relevant discipline is considered as evidence of the implementation of such a policy. Where the required information, according to the patient's presenting complaints/condition is not documented, the score will be adjusted accordingly. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Only those individuals permitted by applicable laws, regulations and professional registration perform the assessments.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit, which will focus on the legibility of signatures and the identification of designations/qualifications. The absence of notes made by the doctor in the patient record, or other relevant clinical personnel other than nursing personnel will affect the outcome of the audit process.

criteria.criterion_text: The scope and content of assessment by each discipline is defined.

It is accepted that the scope and content of assessment for each discipline is defined if the organisation makes use of standardised formats of clinical assessment forms for specific use by different disciplines. The absence of medical notes or other relevant clinical personnel other than nursing personnel in the patient record will affect the outcome of the audit process.Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.It is accepted that the scope and content of assessment for each discipline is defined if the organisation makes use of standardised formats of clinical assessment forms for specific use by different disciplines. The absence of medical notes or other relevant clinical personnel other than nursing personnel in the patient record will affect the outcome of the audit process.Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.
Assessments are performed within appropriate time frames and are comprehensively documented in the patient's records according to organisation policy.
criteria.guideline: Time frames for the assessment of patients should be established for each discipline, for example, there should be an indication of which cases are urgent/or not priority cases. It is important to note that this criterion does not refer to the length of time that it takes to perform the assessment, but the time within which the initial assessment should be commenced and/or completed. These time frames should be stated in the policy framework. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The date and time of assessment can be identified.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

categories.category_title: 2Each patient has an initial assessment which complies with current policies, procedures and guidelines.

standards.standard_title: 3The initial assessment includes a physical examination of the patient.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a nutritional assessment of the patient.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a psychological and behavioral assessment of the patient.
criteria.guideline: Where required, collateral information from outside sources, for example crèches or schools must be included in these assessments. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a developmental history and current functional assessment of the patient.
criteria.guideline: Where required, collateral information from outside sources, for example crèches or schools must be included in these assessments. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a social, cultural and where applicable, economic assessment of the patient and family.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment includes a genogram of family construction and functional assessment of the family.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment results in an understanding of all previous care and the care that the family is presently seeking.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The initial assessment results in a diagnosis and the formulation of an individualised plan of care for the patient and family.
criteria.guideline: If the initial assessment reveals any concern that requires referral to the legal system (for example, neglect or abuse) these must be addressed in the plan of care and implemented according to legal requirements and organisation policy. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation identifies patients in pain during the initial assessment process.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation identifies those patient populations and special situations for which the initial assessment process is modified.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence in the form of a detailed policy and procedure or guideline is provided. To ensure coordination of patient care, all members of the team should be included in the formulation of this policy framework, especially doctors and nurses. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Those special patient populations receive individualised assessments.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The findings of assessments performed outside the organisation are verified on admission.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Any significant changes in the patient’s condition since the assessment performed outside of the organisation are noted in the patient’s record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: A process is in place to identify needs for discharge planning at the initial assessment.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

categories.category_title: 3Healthcare professionals responsible for patient care collaborate to analyse and integrate assessment information.
Patient Care
1Adequate resources are available for the provision of safe care to patients in the service.

standards.standard_title: The care provided to each patient is planned and written in the patient's record.
standards.intent:
A single, integrated plan is preferable to the entry of a separate care plan recorded by each healthcare professional. The care plan should include the care to be delivered and the intended goals of care.
Collaborative care and treatment team meetings or similar patient discussions are recorded. Diagnostic and other procedures must be ordered by individuals qualified to do so. These orders must be easily accessible if they are to be acted on in a timely manner. Locating orders on a common sheet or in a uniform location in patient records facilitates the correct understanding and carrying out of orders. The reason for requesting diagnostic imaging or laboratory tests should be recorded.
An organisation decides:
Which orders must be written rather than verbal
Who is permitted to write orders
Where orders are to be located in the patient record
The method used must respect the confidentiality of patient care information for example, order books must not be kept in an area where they can be seen by other patients or visitors and verbal orders, including those given over the telephone, should be taken in an area where they cannot be overheard.

criteria.criterion_text: The care for each patient is planned, provided and noted in the patient's record.
criteria.guideline: ROOT The need for ongoing education and educational support must be included when planning the care of children in the service. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient's response to care and therapeutic interventions is documented in the patient record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Meetings to discuss patient care/treatment regimes are held by the multidisciplinary team at regular intervals as determined by organisational policy and documented in the patient's record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: All procedures and diagnostic tests ordered and performed, are documented in the patient's record.
criteria.guideline: This documented evidence may include nurses' and doctors' notes as well as printed reports, which are filed in the patient record. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The results of procedures and diagnostic tests performed are available in the patient's record.
criteria.guideline: This criterion will be scored PC if the results are not initialled by the doctor, the treatment plan does not indicate action taken, or the nursing notes do not indicate that the doctor was informed. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Orders are found in a uniform location in patient records.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Only those permitted to write orders do so.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Reassessments are performed at appropriate, regular intervals and following a change in the patient’s condition, and documented in the patient's record.
criteria.guideline: Patients should be reassessed according to clinical need. This may be continuous, hourly or daily (as a minimum), depending on the clinical condition of the patient. Any deterioration in a patient’s condition should result in a formal reassessment by an appropriately trained clinician – often a nurse in the first instance followed by a doctor if clinically indicated. The nature of these reassessments and the processes to be followed should be defined in organisation or departmental policy. If the doctors’ (or other health care practitioners') clinical notes are not available in the patient's records, this criterion will be scored PC. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Care plans are revised when necessary in response to the findings of reassessments.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: All mentally handicapped patients have a physical and dental examination done at least annually.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL  Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

categories.category_title: 3Each patient participates in a structured treatment plan.

standards.standard_title: Compassionate care is provided to the dying and to those in pain.
standards.intent:
While pain may be a part of the patient experience, unrelieved pain has adverse physical and psychological effects. The patient's right to appropriate assessment and management of pain is respected and supported. The organisation has processes to:
Identify patients with pain during initial assessment and reassessment
Communicate with, and provide education for, patients and families about pain management in the context of their personal, cultural and religious beliefs
Educate healthcare providers in pain assessment and management.
Dying patients have unique needs for respectful, compassionate care. Concern for the patient's comfort and dignity guides all aspects of care during the final stages of life. To accomplish this, all personnel are made aware of the unique needs of patients at the end of life. These needs include treatment of primary and secondary symptoms; pain management; response to the psychological, social, emotional, religious and cultural concerns of the patient and family; and involvement in care decisions.
43.3.4Criteria

criteria.criterion_text: The assessment process makes provision for patients in pain to be identified.
criteria.guideline: This requirement should be included in the assessment policy and procedure for the organisation and must include methods for identifying pain in non-communicating and intellectually disabled patients. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients in pain receive care according to pain management guidelines.
criteria.guideline: There must be evidence that all clinical professionals dealing with the patient's pain management have contributed to the patient's record. Monitoring of the effectiveness of pain management must be recorded. Evidence could include the documentation of pain using an individualised assessment form and pain scoring charts. Pain management continues with the prescription and administration of medication for pain as well as the follow-up assessment after treatment. These records will also be evaluated. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence that the effectiveness of pain and symptom management is monitored.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and families are educated about pain and pain management.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation has processes to educate health professionals in assessing and managing pain.
criteria.guideline: Training must include must include the identification of pain in non-communicating and intellectually disabled patients. Documented evidence of personnel training will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel provide respectful and compassionate care to dying patients.
criteria.guideline: This includes the organisation respecting the need of the physically ill or dying patient to be transferred to another suitable facility/unit. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit and patient interviews.

standards.standard_title: Policies and procedures guide the care of high-risk patients and the provision of high-risk services
standards.intent:
Some patients are considered "high-risk" because of their age, condition or the critical nature of their needs. Mentally handicapped patients are commonly in this group as they may not speak for themselves, understand the care process or participate in decisions regarding their care.
Policies and procedures are important for personnel to understand high-risk patients and services, and to respond in a thorough, competent and uniform manner. The clinical and managerial leaders take responsibility for identifying the patients and services considered high-risk, using a collaborative process to develop policies and procedures and to train personnel in their implementation.
Of particular concern is that the policies or procedures identify:
How planning will occur
The documentation required for the care team to work effectively
Special consent considerations
Monitoring requirements
Special qualifications or skills of personnel involved in the care process
Clinical guidelines and pathways are frequently helpful and may be incorporated in the process. Monitoring provides the information needed to ensure that the policies and procedures are adequately implemented and followed for all relevant patients and services.
High risk patients and procedures include (where applicable):
Care of emergency patients
Management of patients in restraint (including the documentation required when a patient is restrained)
Management of patients who may be a danger to themselves or others
Management of the violent patient
Care of frail, dependent and elderly patients
The management of patients in seclusion (including the documentation required when a patient in treated in seclusion)
Observation of patients treated for eating disorders
43.3.5Criteria

criteria.criterion_text: Documented policies, clinical guidelines or standard operating procedures for identified high risk patients and procedures, which include items (a)-(g) in the standard intent above as a minimum, are available and readily accessible.
criteria.guideline: The clinical and managerial leaders take responsibility for identifying the patients and services considered high risk, using a collaborative process to develop policies and procedures and training personnel in their implementation. Implementation will be verified during the patient record audit.  The 40/80 rule for compliance will apply.

criteria.criterion_text: In-service training is provided to personnel to ensure they understand the intent and content of the policies and procedures.

Where policies and procedures refer to patient care, compliance will be measured against the relevant areas in the patients’ records during the record audit process.By implication, if the policies are implemented, personnel can be considered to have been trained and this criterion will be scored accordingly. This training could take the form of formal in-service training, introduction at orientation to policies, discussions at departmental meetings, case studies, etc.Where policies and procedures refer to patient care, compliance will be measured against the relevant areas in the patients’ records during the record audit process.By implication, if the policies are implemented, personnel can be considered to have been trained and this criterion will be scored accordingly. This training could take the form of formal in-service training, introduction at orientation to policies, discussions at departmental meetings, case studies, etc.
Patients receive care consistent with the policies and procedures.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit and patient interviews.

categories.category_title: 6Adequate information is provided when obtaining informed consent from patients or their legal representatives.
1Medication is ordered according to organisational policy and stored in a secure and clean environment.

standards.standard_title: 3Medication is stored in a locked storage device or cabinet that is accessible only to authorised personnel.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication identified for special control by legislation or organisational policy is stored in a cabinet of substantial construction, for which only authorised personnel have the keys.
criteria.guideline: Country-specific laws and regulations or organisational policy will determine the nature of such medication.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication identified for special control by legislation or organisation policy is accurately accounted for.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL Compliance will be measured against the local medication control regulations. Control measures generally include the keeping of registers for these items.

criteria.criterion_text: Medication is securely and legibly labelled with relevant information as required by legislation and organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL Country-specific regulations apply, but must include at least the following: Name of ward and/or name of patient and patient record number (if applicable) Proprietary name/approved name or name of each active ingredient Direction with regard to the manner in which medication must be used Number of dose units in the container Date of dispensing Expiry date and batch number Additional labels with warnings and storage instructions in accordance with organisation policy

criteria.criterion_text: Medication is stored in a clean environment.
criteria.guideline: This applies to all medication storage areas in the organisation.

criteria.criterion_text: A dedicated refrigerator is available for medication requiring storage at low temperatures.
criteria.guideline: There should be a refrigerator designated for the storage of medication only.

criteria.criterion_text: The temperature of the refrigerator is monitored and recorded according to organisational policy.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL Documented evidence in the form of daily temperature recording and monitoring of the refrigerator/s must be available. The temperature must be maintained within a safe range according to manufacturer's specifications. This is usually 2-8 degrees Celsius.

criteria.criterion_text: Appropriate action is taken and recorded when the temperature of the refrigerator is outside the recommended range.
criteria.guideline: The actions taken must be documented and kept on file for reference purposes. Monitoring logs should demonstrate more frequent temperature checks until it is confirmed that the action taken has been successful in restoring maintenance of the required refrigerator temperature range.

criteria.criterion_text: Expiry dates (including those of emergency medication) are checked regularly at defined intervals according to organisational policy and medication is replaced before the expiry date.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured against relevant laws and organisation policies.

categories.category_title: 2Medication use throughout the organisation complies with applicable laws and regulations.
1Food and nutrition therapy appropriate for the patient and consistent with his  or her clinical care is regularly available.
1Each patient's educational needs are assessed and written in their record.

standards.standard_title: Education methods consider the patient's and family's values and
standards.intent:
Learning occurs when attention is paid to the methods used to educate patients and families. The organisation selects appropriate educational methods and people to provide the education.
Personnel collaboration helps to ensure that the information patients and families receive is comprehensive, consistent, and as effective as possible.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient and family are taught in a language and format that they can understand.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews and patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: Those who provide education have the knowledge and communication skills for effective education.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews and personnel training records.

criteria.criterion_text: Health professionals caring for the patient work collaboratively when appropriate.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Interaction between personnel, the patient and family is noted in the patient's record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Continuity of Care

categories.category_title: 1The organisation designs and implements processes to provide continuity of patient care services within the organisation and coordination among health professionals.
2There is a process known to personnel to refer patients for specialised consultation, investigations and/or treatment at other healthcare organisations.

standards.standard_title: 4Follow-up care based on the findings of investigations and/or consultations performed outside the referring organisation is documented in the patient record.

categories.category_title: 3There is a process to transfer patients to another organisation to meet their continuing needs.

standards.standard_title: 1There is a documented process for transferring patients to other organisations.

criteria.criterion_text: When a patient is transferred to another organisation, the receiving organisation is given a written summary of the patient's clinical condition and the interventions performed by the referring organisation.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: A copy of the transfer summary is available in the patient record.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The transferring organisation documents the transfer in the organisation's patient record.
criteria.guideline: Documentation must include as a minimum: The reason(s) for the transfer Any special conditions related to transfer The condition of the patient before transfer The name of the healthcare organisation receiving the patient.  Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The referring organisation determines that the receiving organisation can meet the patient’s continuing care needs, and establishes arrangements to ensure continuity.
criteria.guideline: This should be done by the departmental management team when referral pathways for the unit are established. These pathways should be documented along with all relevant details, for example, transport arrangements and contact details. The documented pathway should be readily available to personnel who will be responsible for making the decision to transfer patients to another organisation.

standards.standard_title: 6Patients are accompanied and monitored by an appropriately qualified person during transfer.
There is an organised process to discharge patients.
standards.intent:
Care planning should include arrangements to meet the patients' continuing needs after discharge as early in the care process as possible. Instructions for discharge and follow-up visits must be clear and provided in writing.
The discharge summary is one of the most important documents for ensuring continuity of care and facilitating correct management at subsequent visits. Information provided by the organisation may include when to resume daily activities, preventive practices relevant to the patient's condition and, when appropriate, information on coping with disease or disability. At discharge, patients should be alerted to symptoms and signs related to their diagnosis which require urgent medical attention and how to access healthcare services should they develop such symptoms or signs. This process is referred to as "safety-netting".
The discharge summary must contain the following content as a minimum:
The diagnosis of main and significant illnesses and reason for admission
The results of investigations that will influence further management
All procedures performed
All significant findings
The patient's condition at discharge
Discharge medication
Follow-up arrangements where appropriate, including emergency review

criteria.criterion_text: There is a documented process to discharge patients.
criteria.guideline: ROOT This process must include any criteria or conditions that the patient and his/her family need to meet prior to discharge. The documented process must be available in the unit and readily accessible to, and known by organisational personnel.

criteria.criterion_text: Where the initial assessment indicates that discharge planning will be required, planning requirements are included in the patient's care plan and completed as scheduled.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation works with the family, caregivers, healthcare practitioners and agencies outside the organisation to ensure timely and appropriate discharge and continuity of care.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and where appropriate their families or caregivers are given understandable follow-up instructions which are documented in the patient's record.
criteria.guideline: The follow-up instructions may be provided to the patient by the medical practitioner, or by the nurse following instructions from the doctor. The instructions must include as a minimum: Medication information Possible complications Return for follow-up care When and where to obtain urgent care Compliance will be verified through patient record audits and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: A discharge summary, which includes (a)-(g) in the standard intent above as a minimum, is written by the medical practitioner when each patient is discharged.
criteria.guideline: This criterion and the next apply to the discharge summary completed by the medical practitioner and not to the nurses' discharge note. Where a discharge summary has only been partially completed, i.e. does not include all items (a)-(f) it will be scored PC. A nurse's discharge note is not acceptable and is scored NC. The 40/80 rule for compliance will apply. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Each record contains a copy of the discharge summary.
criteria.guideline: This will ensure that the discharge summary is available for follow-up visits. Compliance will be verified during patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: When appropriate the patient is given a copy of the discharge summary.
criteria.guideline: This could be appropriate if the patient's care will be continued outside of the country.

criteria.criterion_text: Quality Improvement

categories.category_title: Quality Improvement

standards.standard_title: A formalised proactive quality improvement approach is maintained
standards.intent:
The senior management team is responsible for ensuring that standards are set throughout the organisation. Within each department or service, unit managers should ensure that standards are set for the particular unit. Departmental or service managers should use available data and information to identify priority areas for quality monitoring and improvement. This should be done in collaboration with the organisation's central quality management structures to ensure coordinated quality improvement activities throughout the organisation.
Quality monitoring is typically applied to high risk, high volume or high cost activities, or areas of concern identified by personnel, patients or visitors. Some examples of activities that may benefit from quality monitoring include:
Patient assessment
Procedures carried out
The use of medication
Medication errors
Patient and family expectations and satisfaction
The following will be evaluated:
The manner in which problems were identified and prioritised in this service for which quality improvement activities were initiated
The processes put in place to resolve the problems
The identification of indicators to measure improvement
The tool(s) used to evaluate these indicators
The monitoring of these indicators and corrective steps taken when goals were not achieved
Graphed and/or tabled results, as appropriate
A once-off project such as acquiring a specific item of equipment will be scored NC. Quality improvement processes not related to the clinical quality of patient care but to the environment within which care is provided, for example, monitoring the checking of the emergency trolley over time, will be scored PC.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a written quality improvement programme for the mentally handicapped service that is developed and agreed upon by the personnel of the service.
criteria.guideline: ROOT  The minimum requirement for compliance will be the availability of evidence of: At least one clinical audit Participation in documentation (patient record) audits Participation in the monitoring of near misses, adverse events and sentinel events

standards.standard_title: 2Indicators of performance are identified to evaluate the quality of treatment and patient care.

criteria.criterion_text: Processes are selected in order of priority for evaluation and improvement in the quality of treatment and care.
criteria.guideline: Quality monitoring is typically applied to high risk, high volume or high cost activities, or areas of concern identified by personnel, patients or visitors.

criteria.criterion_text: The quality improvement cycle includes the monitoring and evaluation of the standards set, and remedial action implemented.
criteria.guideline: The quality improvement cycle does not stop with the implementation of a quality improvement plan. Ongoing monitoring of the process or procedure under consideration is required, with evaluation of the monitoring data collected to ensure that the plan has achieved its intended aim. If not, the quality improvement cycle should be repeated, incorporating the learning from previous attempts, until the desired aim is achieved.

criteria.criterion_text: A documentation audit system is in place.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of such audits must be provided at departmental level. Where the documentation (patient record) audit is an organisation-wide multidisciplinary process, which includes all clinical departments, this criterion will be scored according to the findings of the involvement of each department in the process. Evaluation of results and remedial action taken must be documented as well as maintenance of achieved improvements over time.

criteria.criterion_text: An incident managing system for monitoring near misses/adverse events/sentinel events is implemented, which includes the documentation of responses to recorded incidents and interventions to prevent recurrence of the incident or minimise harm in the event of a recurrence.
criteria.guideline: Participation at department level in the organisation’s overall system of the monitoring of near misses/adverse events/sentinel events will be evaluated. Analysed data, with responses/remedial action related to departmental incidents are required.

criteria.criterion_text: Patient Rights

categories.category_title: Patient Rights

standards.standard_title: The organisation is responsible for providing processes that support
standards.intent: This refers to the implementation of organisational policy on patient and family rights. Compliance will be verified during observation of patient care processes, patient record audits and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: There are processes that support patient and family rights during care.
criteria.guideline: ROOT  The organisation's policies will be evaluated against country-specific legislation, where applicable. The implementation thereof will be evaluated during the auditing of patient records, patient interviews and observation during a walk-about in the department. This applies to all the relevant criteria below.

criteria.criterion_text: There are processes to ensure that care is respectful of the patient's personal values and beliefs.
criteria.guideline: Implementation will be evaluated during the auditing of patient records, patient interviews and observation during a walk-about in the department.

criteria.criterion_text: Measures are taken to protect the patient’s privacy, person and possessions.
criteria.guideline: Confidentiality of patient information and patient privacy must be protected at all times. Standard operating procedures specific to the conditions and processes in the unit must be in place, known to relevant personnel and implemented. Likewise, security measures specific to the unit must be in place to safeguard the patients' person and possessions.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel respect the rights of patients and families to treatment and to refuse treatment.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient record audit and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients are informed of their right not to donate human tissue or to participate in research.
criteria.guideline: It is very important to ensure that this internationally accepted right is implemented in the mental health patient population.

criteria.criterion_text: Prevention and control of infection

categories.category_title: Prevention and control of infection

standards.standard_title: There is a comprehensive infection prevention and control programme
standards.intent: This standard measures the implementation of the organisational policy for infection prevention and control at unit level as well as the identification of unit-specific infection control risks. Personnel must be aware of the policy and there must be evidence that it is implemented throughout the organisation.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a programme, which is implemented, to reduce the risks of healthcare associated infections in patients and healthcare workers.
criteria.guideline: ROOT The programme must include as a minimum: Prevention of the spread of respiratory tract infections Prevention of the spread of urinary catheter related infections Prevention of the spread of infection through surgical wounds Prevention of the spread of infection through intravascular/invasive devices Safe injection practices, including single-use injection devices Care of the infected patient The wearing of personal protective equipment The disinfection of equipment Management of infected/soiled linen Management of hazardous waste

criteria.criterion_text: The department participates in the overall programme for quality management and improvement of infection control.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by evidence of departmental representation at organisational infection control meetings and activities and by the departmental contribution of data to the organisational infection control data collection systems.

criteria.criterion_text: The department identifies the procedures and processes associated with the risk of infection, and implements strategies to reduce risk.
criteria.guideline: The department participates in, and has documented evidence of, the identification of risks in their department. The strategies to reduce risk should be defined in policies and procedures and include the risks listed in this standard as a minimum. Such documentation must form part of the organisation-wide infection control processes.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel are trained in correct hand washing procedures.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of personnel training will be required, together with evidence of monitoring of implementation, for example, hand washing audits performed regularly according to organisational policy.

criteria.criterion_text: Organisational policy on handling, storage and disposal of healthcare waste is implemented.
criteria.guideline: The implementation of the organisation's master policy will be evaluated in the department. The processes must include handling at source, collection, storage and disposal of healthcare waste.

criteria.criterion_text: Individuals who collect specimens are trained in the proper collection and handling of microbiological specimens.
